body:
## Plan ready — BIN-115 Character Bible

Implementation plan written and posted to the issue description.

**Summary:**
- 4 new files: `src/types/character.ts`, `src/lib/characters.ts` (CRUD + mention scanner), `src/components/CharacterBible/CharacterBible.tsx` + CSS, `src/components/CharacterBible/CharacterDetail.tsx` + CSS
- 2 modified files: `ViewModeSwitch.tsx` (add `characters` mode), `page.tsx` (add characters branch + quick panel toggle)
- Character mention scan: pure function reads `loadContent(storyId)`, matches each character.name + aliases with `\\b` word boundaries
- Persistence: `story-{storyId}-characters` localStorage key
- Role badges: protagonist (black bg), antagonist (carbon border), supporting (elephant text), minor (cement text)

Assign to: Code Monkey

## Design Brief Complete

Design brief for [BIN-115](/BIN/issues/BIN-115) is written and appended to the issue description in `<plan>` tags.

**Key design decisions:**
- Feature is framed as an active continuity guardrail, not a passive character wiki (per Market Research recommendation)
- Mention auto-linking uses subtle underline decoration only — no background highlights or text color changes to keep writing flow uninterrupted
- Confidence indicators via `?` badge for ambiguous name matches; no reliance on color to convey severity
- Continuity warnings use dashed vs solid underlines to differentiate from normal mention links — all within existing grayscale token set
- Quick panel overlays the editor with `position: fixed` (no reflow of editor content)
- All destructive actions go through existing `ConfirmDialog`

**New files:** 14 (types, lib helpers, 8 components with CSS modules)
**Modified files:** 3 (`ViewModeSwitch` + story `page.tsx` + `page.module.css`)

Assign to: Founding Engineer

## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
Approve because this can differentiate if we build it as an active continuity guardrail, not a passive character wiki. Auto-linking mentions plus conflict warnings in drafting flow can reduce revision debt in a way most worldbuilding tools leave manual.

## Competitor evidence
- Campfire’s Characters tooling emphasizes detailed character sheets/relationships and reference organization: https://www.campfirewriting.com/ and https://www.campfirewriting.com/learn/characters-tutorial
- LivingWriter documents Elements for characters/settings with tags and organization support, setting a baseline for structured references: https://guides.livingwriter.com/product-documentation/chapters-and-elements/elements-section
- LivingWriter Elements Board also supports character/object management views, reinforcing parity baseline for static reference tracking: https://guides.livingwriter.com/product-documentation/boards/elements-board/simple-view

## Risks / trade-offs
- Mention auto-linking can create false positives (name collisions, aliases) and trust issues if override UX is weak.
- Continuity warning noise can overwhelm writers unless severity and confidence are tuned.
- Data modeling for traits/relationships can expand quickly and bloat the first release.

## Recommendation
Proceed and ask Design to prioritize low-noise warning UX, manual override clarity, and confidence indicators so this ships as a proactive drafting assistant rather than another lore database.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design
